mechanism_narrative: Increases in industrial production reflect expanding supply capacity and rising output, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return following any monthly increase in INDPRO is negative in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling period, or the correlation between INDPRO changes and forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 1,000 units per month indicates robust residential investment and consumer demand, leading to economic acceleration and positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return following HOUST > 1000 is not significantly greater than the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.10) over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in unemployment and increases in payroll employment tighten the labor market, raising wage costs and compressing corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE month-over-month decreases and PAYEMS month-over-month increases for at least two consecutive months
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return distribution following a labor tightening trigger is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the average return is above zero.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP is above its 2-year trailing average, further increases reflect sustained demand momentum that supports earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return following increases in GDP above trend is not significantly above the unconditional mean, or the strategy produces negative Sharpe ratio over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices erode the real value of future corporate earnings and increase discount rate uncertainty, leading to negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between monthly CPIAUCSL changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not negative over any 10-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above its trailing 12-month average by at least 25 basis points, it signals a monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates, reducing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day return following trigger events is not negative, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero (<0.5) and the unemployment rate is falling rapidly (decline >0.3pp per month), the Fed is perceived as behind the curve, raising expectations of future tightening that depresses equities over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return following the joint trigger condition is not negative on average, or is not significantly lower than the baseline unconditional return (permutation test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is more than 5% above its 2-year historical average, further increases indicate supply bottlenecks and overheating that lead to margin compression and negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If following an increase in INDPRO during the overheating regime, the 20-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time over any 3-year period.
